name: ALASKA AIR GROUP, INC.
business: Alaska Air Group provides scheduled air transportation of passengers and cargo under the Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines brands, serving destinations throughout North America, Latin America, Asia, and the Pacific. The company also operates a regional airline (Horizon) and a loyalty program (Atmos Rewards). The majority of revenue comes from passenger transportation.

exposures.sentence: Alaska and Horizon use Flyways AI, a technology which leverages artificial intelligence to enable more fuel-efficient flight paths.

We advance SAF through a multi-faceted approach that includes entering into offtake agreements, investing in research and emerging technology, partnering with fuel producers, engaging our guests, and advocating for public policies that support SAF adoption.
Replacing our ground service equipment through the acquisition and use of electric and other lower-emissions equipment also improves operational efficiency.
ZeroAvia is a company developing hydrogen-electric powertrain technology for regional aircraft.
JetZero is a company developing a blended wing body using existing engine technology while delivering up to 50% better fuel efficiency.
In 2025, we began installing Starlink Wi-Fi across our fleet, providing faster and more reliable inflight connectivity.
Cybersecurity threats have and will continue to impact our business.
We are continually exploring how the market develops for high-quality, durable carbon credits from carbon offsets and removals projects to refine our plans to achieving net zero carbon emissions.

catalysts.sentence: We will serve Europe beginning in spring 2026.

catalysts.event: oneworld benefits extend to Hawaiian-branded aircraft
catalysts.sentence: Starting in spring 2026, Alaska's one world benefits will extend to guests traveling on Hawaiian-branded aircraft.
We expect to fully transition the airlines' operations to a single PSS in spring 2026.
Fleetwide installation is expected to be completed by the end of 2027.
Horizon had firm orders to purchase three E175 aircraft with deliveries in 2026.
Alaska also has an agreement with SkyWest Airlines to expand our long-term capacity purchase agreement by one aircraft in 2026.
